name: RBC Summer Tech Labs Innovation Developer
short_description: Join a paid 8-week tech internship building prototypes using JavaScript, GitHub, and REACT.
long_description: Grade 11 and 12 students work in teams of four to solve real business problems for RBC. This is a competitive, paid program located in downtown Toronto with a hybrid work model. Candidates should have a passion for computer science and engineering. Hardware is provided by the bank.

name: STEM Fellowship REO Program
short_description: Conduct hands-on research and shadow lab personnel at university research facilities.
long_description: The Research Exploration Opportunity (REO) program pairs high school students with graduate student mentors. Participants visit first-class labs, learn scientific techniques, and contribute to active experiments. Priority is given to traditionally underrepresented groups in STEM.

name: Sunnybrook Focused Ultrasound Summer Intern
short_description: Assist researchers in the Focused Ultrasound Lab with engineering, technology, and medical projects.
long_description: This is a highly competitive, paid summer internship for Grade 11 and 12 students. Interns work on engineering design, experimental labs, and programming within the FUS Lab. Applicants must have strong academic standing in math and science. The program includes a full-time commitment for July and August.

name: Movia Media Hardware Engineering Intern
short_description: Gain hands-on experience with GPS impression analytics and hardware components for outdoor advertising.
long_description: High school students with an interest in computer engineering assist with hardware testing and data analytics. This winter internship provides exposure to proprietary impression technology and computer networking. Students work alongside professional engineers in a flexible environment.

name: Prepr DevOps and Machine Learning Intern
short_description: Learn to use AI tools and machine learning frameworks while supporting software development projects.
long_description: This virtual internship allows students to gain hands-on experience in DevOps and data science. Participants work on real-world projects, helping to operationalize AI for community benefit. It is an ideal role for students with foundational coding skills looking for professional mentorship.

name: Exiger AI Data Engineer Intern
short_description: Explore how AI and natural language processing are used to safeguard digital assets and supply chains.
long_description: This paid internship is designed for senior high school students with strong foundations in data science. Interns assist with data cleansing, NLP training, and operationalizing AI workflows. The role provides unique insight into the intersection of cybersecurity and artificial intelligence.

name: Toronto International Leadership Centre Intern
short_description: Support the delivery of capacity development programs for central bank and regulatory leaders.
long_description: This administrative internship provides exposure to international leadership development. The intern assists with daily operations, event logistics, and program research. It is a high-level role for students interested in economics, international relations, and public policy.

name: Shad Canada STEAM Leader
short_description: Spend one month at a university campus exploring STEAM and entrepreneurship with top faculty.
long_description: Shad is a prestigious, month-long summer enrichment program for Grade 10 and 11 students. Participants engage in university-level lectures, design challenges, and collaborative projects. The program fosters a community of changemakers and innovators. Includes residential and digital options.

name: UofT Daniels Design Discovery Participant
short_description: Explore architecture and design through hands-on workshops and studio work at UofT.
long_description: This program at the John H. Daniels Faculty of Architecture provides an introduction to creative thinking. Students develop skills in design tools and techniques, building a foundation for studies in architecture. Suitable for students interested in the arts and built environment.

name: AKFC Youth Leadership Academy Delegate
short_description: A fully funded 5-day program for teens to explore global citizenship and sustainable development.
long_description: Students aged 16–18 gather at York University to develop leadership skills and address global challenges. Participants commit to implementing an action project in their community. The program covers all costs for selected delegates. It is a highly selective and prestigious opportunity.

name: LetsStopAIDS Pitch Competitor
short_description: Apply for funding and receive mentorship to launch your own youth health advocacy project.
long_description: "The Winning Seed" pitch competition encourages youth to develop innovative solutions for HIV prevention. Winners receive financial support and guidance from a global youth-led charity. It is an entrepreneurial approach to community service and public health.

name: Starlings Community Youth Advocate
short_description: Participate in peer-informed advocacy for youth affected by parental substance use.
long_description: Youth aged 16–30 with lived experience collaborate on human rights advocacy and letter-writing projects. The program provides a safe space for social justice work and systemic change. It requires a 2-hour weekly commitment. Participants develop skills in writing and organizing.

name: River Street Endodontics Clinic Intern
short_description: Gain experience in dental clinic administration and patient care coordination.
long_description: This internship is ideal for students planning to pursue careers in healthcare or dentistry. Interns assist with patient check-ins, monitoring scheduling platforms, and maintaining infection prevention procedures. It provides hands-on experience in a high-quality clinical environment.

name: Women & Children Social Network Leader
short_description: Attend leadership conferences and manage community initiatives for women and youth.
long_description: Student volunteers attend the mandatory Youth Leadership Conference and lead local advocacy projects. The program focuses on building social capital and supporting vulnerable populations. It is a hybrid role involving both virtual coordination and in-person events in Mississauga and Toronto.

name: CivicAction Youth CivicCorps Team
short_description: Work in project teams to address local housing and affordability crises in the GTHA.
long_description: Youth CivicCorps places volunteers aged 18–30 (including senior high school students) with host organizations. Teams work on marketing, research, or advocacy projects related to economic inclusion. The program includes topical training and well-being resources.

name: Earth Rangers Teens Action Member
short_description: Lead environmental action projects at home and in the community as part of a national network.
long_description: Youth aged 13–18 join a community of environmental advocates. Participants complete projects such as pollinator garden creation or energy conservation and log their impact online. The program offers virtual networking and leadership within the Earth Rangers organization.

name: On Your Mark Online Tutor
short_description: Provide academic support to secondary students in math, science, and literacy via Zoom.
long_description: This program recruits tutors for the 2025/2026 school year. Tutors commit to two hours per week (after-school or Saturdays) to help students from the TCDSB and TDSB. Minimum age for tutors is 17. The program operates in an entirely online format.

name: Kids Help Phone Event Volunteer
short_description: Support high-impact fundraising events like the BMO Walk So Kids Can Talk.
long_description: Volunteers assist with event registration, route marshalling, and guest services. This short-term opportunity supports the mental health of youth across Canada. It is ideal for students needing 8+ hours in a single weekend. Participants join a large, high-energy community of volunteers.

name: Mackenzie Health Summer Student Volunteer
short_description: Volunteer at Cortellucci Vaughan or Richmond Hill hospitals in patient-facing roles.
long_description: Students who have completed Grade 10 can apply for this structured summer term. Volunteers assist with wayfinding, registration, and patient companionship. A minimum commitment of one shift per week for the duration of the summer is required. Recruitment begins in March.

name: Mackenzie Health Year-Round Volunteer
short_description: Support hospital operations and patient experience through weekly shifts in York Region.
long_description: This ongoing program requires a commitment from September until June. Volunteers work in both clinical and non-clinical areas, helping patients navigate the facility and providing administrative support. Students develop professional communication skills in a healthcare environment.

name: Mackenzie Health High School Co-op Intern
short_description: Earn academic credit while exploring healthcare careers through a hospital placement.
long_description: Students apply through their school's co-op office for placements in departments such as Communications, Finance, or Human Resources. Placements provide immersive, hands-on experience and exposure to medical professionals. This is a non-clinical role focused on hospital operations.

name: Halton Healthcare Youth Volunteer
short_description: Support patients and staff at Georgetown, Milton, or Oakville hospitals through companionship.
long_description: Volunteers aged 14+ participate in roles like the Elder Life Program and Gift Shop service. A minimum commitment of 50 hours or 6 months is required. Placements offer exposure to a clinical environment and help enhance the patient experience through compassion and respect.

name: Halton Healthcare High School Co-op Intern
short_description: Gain pre-professional experience in a hospital setting through local co-op offices.
long_description: Coordinated through local school boards, this program places students in specific hospital departments. Students learn about the healthcare system while fulfilling their co-op requirements. Placements vary per semester and provide significant insight into clinical and administrative pathways.

name: Halton Healthcare Elder Life Volunteer
short_description: Provide companionship and socialization to elderly patients to prevent clinical decline.
long_description: Part of a specialized program at Oakville Trafalgar Hospital, volunteers visit seniors to play games, read, and engage in conversation. This role is ideal for students interested in geriatrics or social work. It requires a consistent weekly commitment and mandatory health screening.

name: Joseph Brant Hospital Student Volunteer
short_description: Support patient care and wayfinding at Burlington’s acute care community hospital.
long_description: Volunteers assist with wayfinding, outpatient clinics, and the "Eating Matters" dining program. A 6-month commitment of one shift per week is required. High school students are exempted from the vulnerable sector check fee. Placements focus on creating a positive experience for patients and families.

name: Joseph Brant Hospital Co-op Student
short_description: Explore clinical and non-clinical hospital roles through the local school boards.
long_description: Placements are available in Finance, IT, and Admitting. High school students coordinate through their school co-op teacher. The program allows students to cherish everyone's input while gaining exposure to different professionals. Opportunities vary by semester and hospital need.

name: Oshawa Counsellor In Training (C.I.T.)
short_description: Complete leadership theory and gain hands-on camp experience at Oshawa recreation sites.
long_description: This program for youth aged 14–16 includes one week of leadership theory and two weeks of practical experience. Participants develop skills in program planning and behavior management. Successful completion prepares students for future employment as camp staff. Registration opens in April.

name: Oshawa Leader In Training (L.I.T.)
short_description: Gain foundational leadership skills in a recreation setting for youth aged 12–14.
long_description: The LIT course includes lessons on group management, communication, and game planning. Participants then apply these skills in a camp environment under staff supervision. It is the first step in the city’s youth leadership pipeline. Registration opens in April.

name: Ajax YEAC Committee Member
short_description: Represent youth interests and collaborate on community donation programs in Ajax.
long_description: The Youth Engagement Advisory Committee (YEAC) advises Town Council on youth-related issues and events. Members lead initiatives like the Winter Care Package program for youth in need. Applications open in the late summer for a September–June term.

name: Oakville Youth Action Committee (OYAC)
short_description: Shape municipal youth programming and plan community events as a teen leader.
long_description: OYAC is a leadership group for students aged 13–18 who meet monthly to advise the Town on youth issues. Members plan local events and contribute to the development of recreational facilities. Participation earns community service hours. Meetings held at QE Park Community Centre.

name: Halton Hills AVIP Aquatics Volunteer
short_description: Gain experience as a swim instructor assistant while working toward certifications.
long_description: The Aquatics Volunteer Incentive Program (AVIP) helps youth reduce costs of becoming a certified swim instructor. Volunteers assist staff in delivering lessons and must have completed Swimmer Level 6. It is a pipeline for future employment with the Town.

name: CLOCA CATS Trail Steward
short_description: Help maintain local trails and monitor forest health as a volunteer steward in Durham.
long_description: The Conservation Area Trail Stewards (CATS) program engages students in environmental maintenance. Volunteers monitor trail conditions, report issues, and assist with light maintenance tasks. This role provides significant outdoor experience and contributes to regional conservation goals.

name: RMG Learning & Engagement Volunteer
short_description: Support art instructors in delivering creative programming to children at Durham's art gallery.
long_description: Volunteers assist with daily art activities, games, and supervision during RMG's summer camps and educational programs. It is an excellent way to gain experience working with children and learn about artistic techniques. Participants must be 16+.

name: Girls Inc Durham CIT Program Leader
short_description: Develop leadership skills and mentor younger girls at summer camps in Durham region.
long_description: Youth aged 13–16 co-facilitate activities and build self-confidence. The program focuses on STEM, stress management, and financial literacy. CITs work under staff guidance and earn community service hours. Multiple locations available in Pickering, Whitby, and Oshawa.

name: Habitat for Humanity Durham Youth Builder
short_description: Participate in youth-friendly build days or create gifts for new homeowners.
long_description: Youth aged 10–18 volunteer alongside Habitat staff to decorate gifts for homebuyers or assist with small construction tasks. This program provides insight into affordable housing and community impact. Registration is via group interest forms or seasonal open calls.

name: WE GROW FOOD Adopt-a-Garden Volunteer
short_description: Maintain a school or community garden through watering and weeding during the summer.
long_description: Volunteers are matched with a garden in their neighbourhood and commit to 1–2 hours per week. This flexible, independent role is ideal for students who enjoy being outdoors. Training sessions are held in April. Students may support their own school's garden.

name: Durham Outlook Kitchen Volunteer
short_description: Help prepare and serve daily meals to individuals in need in Oshawa.
long_description: Volunteers work in the kitchen to prepare food or in the dining room to serve guests. This front-line role provides direct support to vulnerable populations in Oshawa. Morning and afternoon shifts are available. Students learn about food security and community support.

name: Halton Food For Thought Student Volunteer
short_description: Prepare and serve healthy snacks in Halton schools to support student nutrition.
long_description: Volunteers work within their own schools to ensure students have access to healthy food. This role involves morning shifts (7:30 - 9:00 AM). Volunteers must undergo screening and follow food safety guidelines. It is a critical support role for child health and learning.

name: Food4Kids Halton Food Bag Delivery
short_description: Deliver food bags to local schools to support students facing food insecurity.
long_description: Volunteers aged 16+ assist with school-year food deliveries in Burlington and Oakville. This role requires a reliable commitment to bi-weekly shifts. It is a direct community service opportunity that ensures children have food for the weekend. A criminal record check is mandatory.

name: Helson Gallery Art Docent
short_description: Facilitate conversations about art and guide visitors through gallery exhibitions.
long_description: Docents aged 16+ help ensure visitors have a valuable experience by explaining art themes. Ideal for students with an interest in art history or public speaking. Training provided on the gallery's permanent collection. Located in the Halton Hills Cultural Centre.

name: Vaughan PL Reading Buddies Volunteer
short_description: Mentor elementary students and help them develop reading skills at library branches.
long_description: Teens in Grade 9–12 act as positive role models, practicing reading and playing literacy games with younger children. Volunteers must complete an interview and attend training. Sessions run throughout the school year. Application packages at service desks.

name: Vaughan PL Makers With a Cause Participant
short_description: Earn volunteer hours by participating in DIY activities to support local charities.
long_description: Students create meaningful items like cards for seniors or blankets for shelters. Held at the Maple Library branch, these sessions provide a creative outlet for community service. Participants must bring school volunteer forms. Registration via Eventbrite.

name: Achēv EmpowHER Program Member
short_description: Join a leadership and civic engagement program for young women and LGBTQ2+ youth.
long_description: Hybrid program for youth aged 15–29. Participants complete 120 hours focused on civic action, project management, and tech skills. Provides mentorship to help youth excel in community leadership. Achēv is a not-for-profit providing newcomer and youth services.

name: Pathways to Education Tutor
short_description: Provide academic support in core subjects to local high school students.
long_description: Tutors commit to one evening per week to assist students with homework and skill-building. The program focuses on helping youth graduate and transition to post-secondary studies. Volunteers must specialize in math, science, English, or French. Located in Regent Park.

name: BGC Durham Learn On Academic Mentor
short_description: Provide homework help and career workshops to peers at Durham youth hubs.
long_description: Operates at Eastview and Northview units in Oshawa. Student mentors assist peers with studies while accessing technology and post-secondary resources. Free drop-in hub on weekday evenings. Fosters a supportive educational community for Durham youth.

name: Mississauga Library TAG Member
short_description: Volunteer for a Teen Advisory Group to help shape library services and collections.
long_description: TAG members provide feedback on library programs and assist with local branch events. Participation earns high school community service hours. It is an ongoing leadership role that encourages civic voice and library advocacy. Available at multiple branches.

name: York Region CAS YAC Member
short_description: Provide a youth voice to the programs and policies of the York Region CAS.
long_description: Council of youth aged 16–23 advocating for rights of children in care. Members meet twice a month on Zoom to consult on agency projects. Participation builds leadership, advocacy, and social justice skills. Equity-focused council for those with lived experience.

name: Volunteer Connect Youth Ambassador
short_description: Promote local volunteering opportunities to peers in Markham and Vaughan.
long_description: Ambassadors help spread awareness of the Volunteer Connect database. They represent the organization at community events and high school volunteer fairs. Role focuses on networking, marketing, and peer engagement. Helps build social capital in York Region.

name: Visions of Science Sr Youth Leader
short_description: Mentor students in Grades 10-12 and facilitate hands-on STEM workshops in Toronto.
long_description: Senior youth leaders provide academic and personal mentorship to younger students in underserved communities. They assist in delivering STEM programming and building community networks. Role emphasizes leadership, advocacy, and educational equity. Part of a structured youth development pathway.

name: St. Vincent de Paul Camp Volunteer
organization: Society of St. Vincent de Paul
short_description: Volunteer at a week-long overnight camp for youth and adults with disabilities.
long_description: Includes training in socialization, supervision, and basic physical assistance. Volunteers support campers in swimming, sports, and crafts. The program fosters social interaction, confidence, and independence. Meals and transportation are provided for the week.

name: Nankind Peer Mentor
short_description: Provide companionship and support to children whose parents are affected by cancer.
long_description: Mentors are matched with a child for weekly visits to build trust and meaningful connections. This role provides joy and support to families during difficult times. Specialized training in grief and trauma-informed care is provided. Requires a high level of dependability and empathy.

name: U+ Community Crochet Volunteer
short_description: Create handmade items for local charities while learning about community needs.
long_description: Students earn volunteer hours by crocheting blankets, hats, and scarves for shelters. This hybrid program allows students to work from home while attending virtual workshops on social impact. Finished items are collected and distributed to vulnerable populations in the Peel and Toronto regions.

name: U+ Community Data Analyst Volunteer
short_description: Use data visualization and analysis to support non-profit reporting.
long_description: This role is ideal for students with an interest in statistics and social science. Volunteers help process data related to community engagement and project outcomes. It is a virtual role that provides practical experience in data engineering and research for the non-profit sector.

name: U+ Community Website Developer
short_description: Support the design and maintenance of non-profit web platforms for youth engagement.
long_description: Students with coding skills assist in building and updating community websites. This is a high-impact technical volunteer role that provides professional-level web development experience. Volunteers work under the guidance of tech mentors to ensure platforms are accessible and secure.

name: Prepr Digital Marketing Intern
short_description: Draft and schedule social media content while learning about the Future of Work.
long_description: This virtual internship focuses on digital literacy and professional communication. Interns manage LinkedIn and Instagram channels for a leading tech non-profit. They receive training in SEO, content strategy, and platform analytics. The role is designed to reward initiative with meaningful equity.

name: BGC Durham BYTE Lab Coding Mentor
short_description: Assist Black girls with foundational coding and AI game creation projects in Oshawa.
long_description: Mentors work in partnership with Ontario Tech Engineering Outreach to deliver coding workshops. Role involves guiding students through machine learning concepts and creative tech projects. It provides significant leadership experience in an academic and community integration setting.

name: Across U-hub Youth Program Assistant
short_description: Assist with the delivery of cultural and social programs for youth in Markham.
long_description: Student assistants help organize workshops, cultural festivals, and community outreach events. Role provides exposure to non-profit management and community development. May require work on weekends or evenings. Supports a safe and inclusive hub for York Region youth.

name: Oak Ridges Trail Steward
short_description: Help maintain trail conditions and monitor forest health in the Aurora/Whitby area.
long_description: Junior stewards monitor specific segments of the Oak Ridges Moraine trail. Responsibilities include reporting maintenance issues, removing litter, and assisting with trail restoration events. Provides significant outdoor experience and insight into environmental management.

name: Keep Durham Beautiful Litter Leader
short_description: Lead community cleanup events and advocate for waste reduction in Durham Region.
long_description: Litter Leaders are trained to organize their own local cleanups and educate peers on environmental stewardship. Roles include coordinating volunteers, managing supplies, and tracking litter data for municipal reporting. Ideal for students seeking leadership roles within the environmental sector.

name: Keep Durham Beautiful Exhibit Assistant
short_description: Support the installation of student art exhibits focused on environmental resilience.
long_description: Volunteers assist with the "Ready, Set, Resilience" exhibit at the Museum of Life and Science. Roles include greeting visitors, explaining art themes, and assisting with setup/teardown. Unique intersection of arts administration and environmental advocacy.

name: City of Mississauga MiWay Student Ambassador
short_description: Promote public transit and sustainable travel to peers at Mississauga schools.
long_description: Ambassadors represent MiWay by running information booths and digital marketing campaigns. This professional development role focuses on communications and community outreach. Applications open in the spring for a September start. Earns significant leadership hours.

name: City of Mississauga Animal Shelter Socializer
short_description: Assist with the care and socialization of cats and small animals at the shelter.
long_description: Volunteers help shelter animals become more comfortable with human interaction to increase adoption success. Roles include light cleaning, feline enrichment activities, and promotional support. Requires completion of an orientation and a consistent weekly commitment.

name: City of Mississauga Parks Steward
short_description: Volunteer to maintain public garden beds and woodlands across Mississauga.
long_description: Individual stewards are assigned to specific parks to assist with weeding, mulching, and planting. Role includes training in native plant identification and conservation techniques. Flexible schedule suitable for students looking for independent outdoor work.

name: Halton Hills Tiny Dancers Assistant
short_description: Assist dance instructors with classes for children aged 2 to 6 in Halton Hills.
long_description: Volunteers support recreation staff in delivering ballet, hip hop, and acro lessons. Role focuses on encouraging creativity and confidence in young learners. Ideal for students with dance experience interested in early childhood education or recreation leadership.

name: Helson Gallery Workshop Docent
short_description: Guide gallery visitors and assist with art education workshops in Georgetown.
long_description: Docents facilitate conversations about current exhibitions and support hands-on art activities. Role requires an interest in the arts and strong communication skills. Training is provided on the gallery's permanent collection and exhibition goals.

name: York Region Summer Student Intern
short_description: Gain exposure to the public sector through a 4-month summer internship in Newmarket.
long_description: Students work in departments such as Parks, Finance, or Social Services. The program focuses on aligning career aspirations with professional projects. Applicants must be returning to full-time studies in September. Recruitment occurs in January for May starts.

name: Mackenzie Health Clinical Registry Assistant
short_description: Provide administrative support to clinical departments in Vaughan or Richmond Hill.
long_description: Volunteers assist with filing, data entry, and organizing medical records within a hospital setting. This non-clinical role provides significant insight into the administrative operations of a large healthcare network. Requires a high level of confidentiality and reliability.

name: Joseph Brant Hospital Friendly Visitor
short_description: Provide companionship and emotional support to patients in Burlington.
long_description: Friendly visitors spend time with patients playing games, reading, or engaging in conversation to enhance their hospital stay. This role is critical for patient well-being and social interaction. Requires a consistent weekly commitment of 3-4 hours.

name: Joseph Brant Hospital Dining Volunteer
short_description: Assist staff with the Eating Matters program to support patient nutrition.
long_description: Volunteers help set up meal trays and provide encouragement to patients during dining hours. This role ensures patients have a positive mealtime experience and supports nutritional goals. Volunteers do not feed patients but provide social and logistical support.

name: BGC Durham Homework Club Assistant
short_description: Provide academic support and homework help to peers at Oshawa youth units.
long_description: Student assistants mentor younger peers in math, science, and literacy. Held at the Eastview and Northview units, these sessions provide a structured environment for academic success. Ideal for students looking for year-round tutoring experience.

name: BGC Durham Aquatics Instructor Assistant
short_description: Support swimming instructors during lessons and build leadership skills in Oshawa.
long_description: Volunteers assist with class management and demonstrate skills to young swimmers. This role is a pipeline for future employment as a certified lifeguard or instructor. Participants must have an interest in youth development and water safety.

name: Girls Inc Durham STEM Workshop Volunteer
short_description: Assist with the delivery of STEM workshops for girls in Pickering and Whitby.
long_description: Volunteers support facilitators in delivering hands-on experiments and tech projects. The program aims to encourage girls' participation in non-traditional career paths. Roles include project setup, student guidance, and logistical support.

name: Food4Kids Halton Drive Organizer
short_description: Lead a local food drive to collect items for weekend food bags in Burlington.
long_description: Student leaders coordinate the collection of specific high-need items from their schools or neighborhoods. Role involves marketing, logistics, and coordinating with Food4Kids staff. Provides significant project management and community service experience.

name: Halton Healthcare Patient Transport Volunteer
short_description: Assist with the safe movement of patients and equipment within Oakville hospital.
long_description: Volunteers work alongside staff to transport discharged patients and deliver medical supplies. This active role requires good communication skills and the ability to navigate a large clinical facility. Provides unique exposure to hospital logistics.

name: Halton Healthcare Information Desk Volunteer
short_description: Welcome visitors and provide wayfinding assistance at Milton District Hospital.
long_description: Volunteers act as the first point of contact for patients and families, providing directions and answering general inquiries. Role emphasizes customer service and professional communication. Requires a reliable commitment to weekly shifts.

name: Vaughan PL English Conversation Volunteer
short_description: Facilitate conversation circles for newcomers to practice English in Vaughan.
long_description: Student volunteers assist newcomers in improving their speaking skills through casual conversation and literacy games. Role supports social integration and builds cultural awareness. Held throughout the school year at various library branches.

name: Vaughan PL Chess Club Volunteer
short_description: Instruct children in playing chess and improving their game at library branches.
long_description: Teen volunteers share their knowledge of chess with younger children in a supervised club setting. Role involves teaching basic moves, strategy, and facilitating matches. Ideal for knowledgeable chess players looking for community service hours.

name: CMHA York Region Awareness Volunteer
short_description: Support mental health advocacy and awareness campaigns across York Region.
long_description: Volunteers participate in community events and awareness-raising initiatives such as Mental Health in Motion. This role involves public engagement, setting up event spaces, and distributing resources. Ideal for students interested in psychology or public health.

name: Pathways to Education Group Mentor
short_description: Facilitate after-school social and academic activities for students in Regent Park.
long_description: Mentors lead small groups of Grade 9 and 10 students in social development programs. Roles include planning interactive workshops, promoting teamwork, and acting as a positive role model. Commitment is twice a month throughout the school year.

name: Newmarket Library YA Pride Club Leader
short_description: Lead discussion groups and events for LGBTQ2S+ youth in Newmarket.
long_description: Teen leaders co-facilitate a safe space for youth to discuss identity and current events. Responsibilities include planning club meetings, leading icebreakers, and coordinating special library events. Applicants should identify as LGBTQ2S+ or be strong, informed allies.

name: Newmarket Library Reading Buddy Mentor
short_description: Provide one-on-one literacy support to children who require extra reading practice.
long_description: Mentors are paired with elementary students to build reading confidence through games and storytelling. This role requires patience, strong communication, and a commitment of 2 hours per week for 10 weeks. A rewarding experience for students interested in education.

name: Achēv Youth 2 Action (Y2A) Partner
short_description: Design and implement community mapping projects to address social issues in Peel.
long_description: Y2A empowering youth to become active citizens through training in project management and civic engagement. Participants work in teams to identify community needs and launch meaningful solutions. Offers a combination of virtual workshops and in-person action in Brampton.

name: Achēv EmpowOUR Participant
short_description: Engage in a leadership program focused on social equity and community inclusion.
long_description: This program focuses on developing leadership skills through the lens of equity. Participants collaborate on workshops and advocacy campaigns to support diverse populations in Mississauga. It provides a supportive environment for youth to build professional networks and social impact.

name: Ifarada Youth Advisory Board Member
short_description: Advocate for youth-focused mental health interventions and social events.
long_description: Board members meet bi-monthly to review Ifarada’s programs and participate in community events like the Tallawah youth conference. This role focuses on ensuring youth perspectives guide the organization’s support services. Members are compensated for their time and leadership.

name: Halton Police Youth Advisory Council Member
short_description: Provide insight to the HRPS on policies and programs impacting Halton youth.
long_description: YAC members act as an advisory board, identifying opportunities for police to engage with young people. The council meets monthly from September to June to discuss community safety and youth relations. Participation helps ensure youth needs are represented in regional policing.

name: Durham Police CSAC Youth Representative
short_description: Advise police leadership on community perceptions and safety concerns in Durham.
long_description: The Community Safety Advisory Council (CSAC) includes youth representatives who provide feedback on police program delivery. Members act as ambassadors to improve relationships between the DRPS and the diverse communities of Durham Region. Recruitment occurs seasonally for ward-specific roles.

name: Triple Triple Ent Business Plan Writer
short_description: Support startup projects through the creation of business plans and forecasts.
long_description: This internship provides training on modern business tools and real-world startup cases. Interns assist with drafting professional plans used for startup funding and project scaling. Ideal for students interested in entrepreneurship, finance, and professional writing.

name: TCEP Food Educator Volunteer
short_description: Assist with the delivery of food literacy programs and cooking workshops.
long_description: Food educators supervise cooking activities and lead group discussions on nutrition and food security. Role involves program delivery from setup to clean-up at community hubs in Whitby. Offers hands-on experience in community health and cultural empowerment.

name: Keep Durham Beautiful Exhibit Guide
short_description: Educate visitors on environmental resilience at student art exhibits in Durham.
long_description: Guides greet the public and explain the themes of exhibits such as "Ready, Set, Resilience." Responsibilities include maintaining exhibit spaces and gathering feedback from attendees. intersection of arts education and environmental advocacy.

name: Halton Hills Heritage Committee Vol
short_description: Support the preservation and promotion of local heritage sites in Halton Hills.
long_description: Volunteers assist the Heritage Halton Hills Committee with research projects and community outreach events. Role focuses on documenting historical landmarks and educating the public on regional history. Ideal for students interested in history and municipal planning.

name: Ontario Tech Digital Design Participant
short_description: Join virtual workshops to master digital fabrication and 3D modeling using Onshape.
long_description: This series of workshops takes students from basics to advanced assemblies of digital prototypes. Participants design functional objects like phone stands and multi-part devices. The program provides a technical foundation in computer-aided design for future engineering studies.

name: Ontario Tech Inside Engineering Scholar
short_description: Explore university-level engineering concepts through hands-on project challenges.
long_description: Participants engage in capstone project challenges designed to mirror university learning styles. The program provides real-world insights from industry professionals and fosters skills in critical thinking and innovation. Includes a final showcase session to present designs.

name: Mackenzie Health Geriatric Admin Vol
short_description: Provide administrative support to the geriatric care and rehab units in Vaughan.
long_description: Volunteers assist with patient registry, filing, and office logistics in a specialized geriatric setting. This role provides exposure to the unique healthcare needs of seniors and the administration of clinical support. Requires reliable weekly commitment and health screening.

name: Halton Healthcare Retail Assistant
short_description: Support hospital fundraising by serving customers in hospital gift shops.
long_description: Volunteers assist with sales and customer service in retail spaces that raise funds for medical equipment. Roles include restocking items, assisting visitors with purchases, and handling simple transactions. A community-focused way to support hospital development.

name: Joseph Brant Hospital IT Support Vol
short_description: Assist the hospital IT department with basic hardware and software logistics.
long_description: Student volunteers support the technology team with inventory management and basic equipment preparation. This non-clinical role provides insight into the digital infrastructure required for modern patient care. Ideal for students with an interest in computer science and healthcare.

name: U+ Community Senior Speaking Mentor
short_description: Lead public speaking workshops and debates for peers in the Peel Region.
long_description: Senior mentors facilitate weekly club meetings, providing feedback on speech techniques and debate strategy. The role focuses on building communication skills and confidence in a virtual peer environment. Volunteers are responsible for planning club themes and judging matches.

name: Visions of Science STEM Mentor
short_description: Facilitate interactive science and tech workshops for youth in Toronto hubs.
long_description: Mentors work at local community spaces to deliver curriculum-based STEM workshops to younger students. Role involves leading hands-on experiments and fostering a passion for scientific inquiry. A great opportunity to support educational equity in priority neighbourhoods.

name: Society of St. Vincent de Paul Tutor
organization: Society of St. Vincent de Paul
short_description: Provide after-school homework help to youth in underserved Toronto areas.
long_description: Tutors assist students with math, science, and literacy on weekday evenings. The program aims to support academic success and provide a safe learning environment for children. Volunteers must be at least 17 years old and commit to one shift per week.

name: Citizen Relations PR Intern
short_description: Join a leading PR agency to support media relations and influencer marketing projects.
long_description: Interns assist with writing pitches, identifying influencers, and brainstorming creative campaign ideas. The role provides an immersive look into the communications industry in downtown Toronto. This is a full-time, paid opportunity for students interested in marketing.

name: Halton Food For Thought Social Leader
short_description: Coordinate student nutrition awareness campaigns within your high school.
long_description: Social leaders advocate for food security by running digital campaigns and coordinating local snack programs. Role involves creative marketing and peer leadership to ensure students have access to healthy meals. Training and resources provided to help scale school-based projects.

name: Food4Kids Halton School Drive Lead
short_description: Lead a team of students to organize a high-impact food drive for local peers.
long_description: Leads are responsible for the marketing, collection, and delivery logistics of school-based food drives. This role provides significant project management experience and helps fight food insecurity in Burlington and Oakville. Direct coordination with Food4Kids staff required.

name: CMHA York Region Philanthropy Vol
short_description: Support fundraising and donor relations efforts for mental health programs.
long_description: Volunteers assist with donor database management, event logistics, and community outreach. This administrative role provides insight into the operation of a major mental health non-profit. Requires a high level of professionalism and strong data management skills.

name: Keep Durham Beautiful Plant Steward
short_description: Join invasive species removal and native planting events across Durham.
long_description: Stewards work in teams to restore local biodiversity at parks such as Wrightwood and CR Wood. Responsibilities include removing invasive plants and planting native trees to enhance urban forest health. This seasonal role provides significant hands-on environmental experience.

name: Mackenzie Health Community Liaison
short_description: Promote hospital volunteer opportunities and events at York Region fairs.
long_description: Liaisons act as ambassadors for Mackenzie Health, representing the hospital at high schools and volunteer recruitment events. Role focuses on public speaking, networking, and spreading awareness of clinical support roles. Ideal for students with strong communication skills.

name: Joseph Brant Hospital Family Liaison
short_description: Act as a communication link between patients' families and surgical waiting areas.
long_description: Liaisons provide updates and comfort to family members while their loved ones are in surgery. This role requires empathy, professionalism, and the ability to maintain confidentiality in a clinical setting. A high-impact role for patient and family experience.

name: U+ Community Camp Planner
short_description: Collaborate with recreation staff to design summer camp curricula in Mississauga.
long_description: Volunteers assist in creating schedules, activities, and educational modules for youth summer camps. This role focuses on program design and leadership within a community hub. Ideal for students interested in early childhood education or recreation management.

name: Pathways to Education Career Mentor
short_description: Support senior high school students in exploring post-secondary and career paths.
long_description: Mentors provide guidance on university applications, scholarship opportunities, and career exploration. The program aims to bridge the gap for students in Regent Park seeking higher education. Requires strong interpersonal skills and academic knowledge.

name: Visions of Science Youth Advocate
short_description: Advocate for STEM education accessibility and represent youth voices in Toronto.
long_description: Advocates collaborate on campaigns to highlight the importance of inclusive STEM learning in underserved communities. Responsibilities include social media outreach, public speaking, and project design. Part of a leadership development pathway within the organization.

name: Halton Hills Acton Youth Hub Vol
short_description: Support the operation of the drop-in youth centre in Acton.
long_description: Volunteers assist with daily activities including gym sports, games nights, and arts/crafts. The role involves creating a safe and welcoming environment for youth aged 12-24. Ideal for students looking for year-round community engagement in North Halton.

name: Newmarket Library Chess Club Mentor
short_description: Lead weekly chess sessions and teach strategies to younger children.
long_description: Mentors facilitate matches, demonstrate opening moves, and ensure a positive competitive environment. This role requires a strong understanding of chess and experience working with children. A great way to share a hobby while earning leadership hours.

name: Keep Durham Beautiful Litter Scout
short_description: Lead independent neighborhood cleanups and track litter data in Ajax.
long_description: Scouts are trained to organize and report on local litter removal efforts. This independent role allows students to impact their community on a flexible schedule while contributing to regional waste data. Participants earn hours based on logged cleanup events.

name: Achēv Event Planning Assistant
short_description: Support the coordination of community workshops and networking events.
long_description: Assistants help with event registration, venue setup, and digital promotion for Achēv's youth and newcomer programs. Role provides exposure to event management and non-profit logistics. Ideally suited for students with strong organizational skills.

name: Ifarada Film Ambassador
short_description: Interview elders and document their experiences for the Mzee Film Project.
long_description: Ambassadors co-produce short films highlighting the lived experiences of community elders. Responsibilities include conducting interviews, basic video editing, and promoting project screenings. This creative role bridges generational gaps and supports cultural heritage.

name: Halton Hills Trail Monitor
short_description: Perform regular inspections and report deficiencies on the Georgetown trail network.
long_description: Trail monitors are assigned specific segments to check for litter, damage, and invasive plant growth. This independent outdoor role supports the successful operation of municipal green spaces. Reports are submitted directly to the recreation department.

name: Mackenzie Health Admitting Volunteer
short_description: Assist patients with registration and wayfinding in the main admitting area.
long_description: Volunteers act as the first point of contact, ensuring patients have a smooth check-in process at Vaughan hospital. Role involves patient navigation and administrative support. Requires professional demeanor and ability to stand for extended periods.

name: Joseph Brant Hospital Day Surgery Vol
short_description: Support the patient experience in the Day Surgery and Endoscopy unit.
long_description: Volunteers assist with non-medical patient needs, ensuring comfort during the pre- and post-operative phases. Role focuses on enhancing patient flow and providing socialization. Requires mandatory health screening and a consistent weekly commitment.

name: U+ Community Brand Ambassador
short_description: Promote youth services and programs through social media and local events.
long_description: Ambassadors represent U+ Community at school fairs and networking events, highlighting available workshops and volunteer roles. Role involves content creation for social channels and peer outreach. Helps build social capital and program awareness in Peel.

name: Visions of Science Jr STEM Leader
short_description: Support the delivery of science projects for younger peers in underserved hubs.
long_description: Jr. Leaders assist senior staff in guiding students through foundational STEM concepts. Role focuses on peer mentorship and building confidence in scientific inquiry. A great entry-level leadership role for students interested in education.

name: Halton Hills Helson Gallery Guide
short_description: Provide exhibition tours and lead art activities at the Helson Gallery.
long_description: Gallery guides promote appreciation of current exhibitions and facilitate hands-on art workshops for visitors. Requires an interest in the arts and strong public speaking skills. Training provided on the gallery's permanent collection.

name: Keep Durham Beautiful Spring Exhibit Vol
short_description: Support the installation and staffing of annual spring art exhibits.
long_description: Volunteers assist with the setup of environmental-themed exhibits and manage guest services during opening events. Role provides insight into arts administration and non-profit outreach. Held annually in the spring at Durham community spaces.

name: Mackenzie Health Patient Partner
short_description: Collaborate with hospital leadership to improve the patient experience.
long_description: Patient partners share their insights and feedback to help shape hospital policies and service delivery. This high-level leadership role ensures that patient voices are integrated into organizational decision-making. Ideal for students with lived experience in the healthcare system.

name: Joseph Brant Hospital Outpatient Vol
short_description: Assist staff and patients in specialized outpatient clinics in Burlington.
long_description: Volunteers support clinic operations including the fracture, oncology, and breastfeeding clinics. Role involves greeting patients, providing simple instructions, and managing clinic flow. A rewarding role for students interested in clinical administration.

name: Achēv Tech Skills Participant
short_description: Join a hybrid program to develop digital literacy and tech-based leadership skills.
long_description: Participants receive training in web design, content creation, and project management. The program aims to equip youth with the tools needed to lead in the digital economy. Includes collaborative projects with community partners in Mississauga.

name: Pathways to Education Academic Leader
short_description: Lead study groups and provide specialized tutoring in core high school subjects.
long_description: Academic leaders focus on higher-level math and sciences, assisting peers with complex concepts and exam prep. Role involves creating study resources and fostering a positive learning culture. Ideally suited for senior students with strong academic backgrounds.

name: Newmarket Library Teen Advisor
short_description: Help shape the library's teen collection and service offerings.
long_description: Teen advisors provide feedback on current library trends and suggest new programs for the youth community. Participation earns community involvement hours and offers a platform for youth voice in municipal services. Meets monthly from October to June.

name: Keep Durham Beautiful Cleanup Lead
short_description: Organize and lead seasonal cleanup events in your local Oshawa neighborhood.
long_description: Cleanup leads are responsible for recruiting volunteers, managing supplies, and tracking litter metrics. This role provides leadership experience within the environmental sector and helps beautify local communities. Support and safety training provided.

name: Mackenzie Health Communications Intern
short_description: Gain experience in hospital communications and public relations in Vaughan.
long_description: Interns assist with social media content, internal newsletters, and event promotion. This role provides insight into the communications strategy of a large regional healthcare network. Coordinated through school co-op offices for academic credit.

name: Joseph Brant Hospital Admitting Vol
short_description: Assist with patient check-ins and registration at Burlington Acute Care.
long_description: Volunteers ensure patients have all required documentation and provide navigation assistance to clinical units. This front-desk role emphasizes customer service and confidentiality in a fast-paced environment. Requires professional communication skills.

name: Achēv Newcomer Youth Mentor
short_description: Support recently immigrated youth in adjusting to life and school in Canada.
long_description: Mentors provide social support and academic guidance to help newcomers navigate their new community. The role focuses on building confidence and foster inclusive connections. Hybrid model with both virtual and in-person components in Brampton.

name: Visions of Science Research Partner
short_description: Collaborate on community-based research projects focused on educational equity.
long_description: Research partners assist in data collection and analysis related to STEM education accessibility. Role involves literature reviews, survey design, and report writing. Provides unique exposure to social science research and advocacy in Toronto.

name: Halton Hills AVIP Pool Leader
short_description: Assist swim instructors and demonstrate water safety skills in Georgetown.
long_description: Pool leaders support the delivery of municipal swimming lessons and act as role models for young swimmers. This role is a pipeline for future employment as a certified instructor or lifeguard. Requires completion of Swimmer Level 6 and AVIP workshop.

name: Keep Durham Beautiful Waste Advocate
short_description: Lead community workshops on waste reduction and recycling in Whitby.
long_description: Advocates develop and deliver presentations to local groups and schools on the impact of litter. Responsibilities include researching waste metrics and creating engaging educational content. ideally suited for students passionate about sustainability.

name: Mackenzie Health Finance Intern
short_description: Gain experience in hospital financial administration and reporting in Richmond Hill.
long_description: Interns assist with budget tracking, invoicing, and administrative support for the finance department. This non-clinical role provides insight into the fiscal operations of a large healthcare network. Coordinated through school co-op offices.

name: Joseph Brant Hospital IT Intern
short_description: Explore healthcare IT and data security through a semester-long internship.
long_description: Interns assist with hardware setup, software troubleshooting, and inventory management within a clinical environment. Role provides practical experience in computer science and hospital digital infrastructure. Coordinated through school board co-op offices.

name: U+ Community Social Media Vol
short_description: Create digital content to highlight community social impact projects in Peel.
long_description: Volunteers design flyers, write blog posts, and manage social media accounts for youth initiatives. This role emphasizes branding, communication, and digital literacy. It is a flexible, virtual opportunity to build a professional portfolio.

name: Pathways to Education Math Tutor
short_description: Provide specialized tutoring in secondary mathematics for students in Regent Park.
long_description: Tutors assist with algebra, calculus, and geometry, helping students master complex concepts and improve academic outcomes. The program aims to increase graduation rates through dedicated subject support. Requires strong math proficiency and commitment.

name: Halton Hills Environmental Steward
short_description: Join local conservation projects to restore biodiversity in Georgetown green spaces.
long_description: Stewards participate in tree planting, invasive plant removal, and trail maintenance events. Role focuses on improving the ecological health of the regional trail network. A great way for students to gain outdoor experience and environmental literacy.

name: Mackenzie Health HR Intern
short_description: Gain experience in healthcare human resources and recruitment in Vaughan.
long_description: Interns assist with applicant screening, onboarding logistics, and administrative support for the HR team. This role provides insight into the staffing and operations of a major regional hospital. Coordinated through school board co-op offices.

name: Joseph Brant Hospital Admin Intern
short_description: Explore healthcare administration through a non-clinical hospital placement.
long_description: Interns assist with filing, registration logistics, and departmental administration in Burlington. This role provides immersive experience in hospital operations and prepares students for careers in management or health sciences. Coordinated through co-op offices.

name: U+ Community CIT Leader
short_description: Mentor younger campers and develop leadership skills at summer hubs in Peel.
long_description: Counsellors-in-Training assist staff in delivering games, activities, and educational modules. The role focuses on group management, conflict resolution, and peer mentorship. A great pathway to future employment in recreation or childcare.

name: Keep Durham Beautiful Data Lead
short_description: Track and analyze litter collection metrics to support municipal reporting.
long_description: Data leads manage the reporting of cleanup outcomes, identifying trends in neighborhood waste. This role focuses on data science within the environmental sector and helps evaluate the impact of regional conservation programs. ideally suited for students with analytical interests.

name: Mackenzie Health Wayfinding Vol
short_description: Help patients and visitors navigate the hospital facility at Richmond Hill.
long_description: Volunteers provide directions, escort guests to clinical units, and answer general inquiries. This role is critical for improving the patient experience and ensuring smooth hospital navigation. Requires a friendly demeanor and consistent weekly commitment.

name: Joseph Brant Hospital Fracture Clinic Vol
short_description: Support staff and patients in the acute care fracture clinic in Burlington.
long_description: Volunteers assist with clinic flow, greeting patients, and providing simple instructions during their visits. This role provides significant exposure to a fast-paced clinical environment. Requires mandatory health screening and reliable weekly attendance.

name: Achēv Civic Action Member
short_description: Join a hybrid program to design advocacy campaigns on local social issues.
long_description: Members collaborate on real-world projects focused on civic engagement and community impact. The program provides training in governance, media, and technology to help youth excel in community leadership. Open to diverse youth populations in Mississauga and Brampton.

name: Visions of Science Hub Coordinator
short_description: Assist with the logistical operation of STEM workshops in Toronto community hubs.
long_description: Coordinators manage equipment setup, student registration, and session logistics. This role focuses on operational support for educational programming in priority neighbourhoods. Provides exposure to non-profit management and community engagement.
